title: Add failure reason to AuthDependencies for better diagnostics
body:
## Background

From PR #268 code review finding.

## Problem

`buildAuthDependencies` returns empty `AuthDependencies` on any error (missing config, token store init failure) but only logs warnings. Callers cannot distinguish between:
- Auth intentionally disabled
- Auth misconfigured
- Auth initialization failed

Users might think auth is working when it has silently failed.

## Proposed Solution

Add a status field to AuthDependencies:

```go
type AuthStatus string

const (
    AuthStatusDisabled      AuthStatus = "disabled"       // Legacy mode
    AuthStatusConfigured    AuthStatus = "configured"     // Platform mode, fully configured
    AuthStatusNotConfigured AuthStatus = "not_configured" // Platform mode, missing config
    AuthStatusError         AuthStatus = "error"          // Initialization failed
)

type AuthDependencies struct {
    TokenStore *auth.TokenStore
    Client     *auth.Client
    Status     AuthStatus
    StatusMsg  string  // Human-readable explanation
}
```

This allows callers to:
1. Show appropriate messages to users
2. Decide whether to fail fast or continue gracefully
3. Provide actionable guidance for misconfigurations

## Impact

Better diagnostics and user experience when auth is misconfigured.

## References

- PR #268
- Finding fingerprint: `0a036c6205d4caed48cbcfeb85d31931`
